title: Red Sauce Pasta
tagline: Fresh marinara from blanched tomatoes — bright, garlicky and better than any jar.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients. This is a genuinely lean, high-volume meal — the tomatoes and soya keep it filling for the calories.
The soya chunks are what make this gym-friendly: they push protein from ~10 g to ~28 g per serving for barely any extra calories.
Skip the parmesan and use 1 tbsp oil instead of 3 to drop another ~120 kcal per serving.
For a non-veg version, drop the soya and toss in 150 g pan-seared chicken breast per serving instead.

items: 5–6 medium tomatoes (~500 g), blanched and puréed
2–3 tbsp olive oil (mustard oil works too)
1/2 tsp dried basil (or a few fresh leaves)
2 tbsp tomato paste (optional, for extra body)
100 g dry soya chunks (50 g per serving)
Grated parmesan and fresh basil, to finish (optional)

steps.title: Blanch and purée the tomatoes
steps.body: Score an 'X' on the base of 5–6 tomatoes, drop into boiling water for 30–60 sec, then into cold water. Peel and blend to a smooth purée. Blending only the tomatoes (not the garlic/onion) keeps the sautéed flavours layered and the sauce richer.

steps.title: Prep the soya
steps.body: Boil 100 g soya chunks in salted water for 5 min, drain, and — this bit matters — squeeze each chunk dry with your hands so they can soak up sauce instead of water. Roughly chop or halve them.

steps.title: Boil the pasta
steps.body: Cook 200 g pasta in well-salted water ('salty like the sea') until al dente, about 1 min less than the packet. Before draining, save 1 cup of pasta water. Do not rinse the pasta.

steps.title: Sauté the aromatics
steps.body: Heat 2–3 tbsp olive oil on medium. Add 4 minced garlic cloves and cook until fragrant; add the chopped onion (if using) and cook until translucent. If using tomato paste, stir in 2 tbsp now and cook 1–2 min to deepen it.

steps.title: Cook the sauce
steps.body: Pour in the tomato purée and add 1/2 tsp chilli flakes, 1/2 tsp oregano and 1/2 tsp basil. Simmer on low 10–15 min, stirring, until it thickens and loses the raw-tomato edge.

steps.title: Season
steps.body: Add 1 tsp salt and 1/2 tsp sugar. Taste and adjust — a pinch of salt at a time. Remember the parmesan (if using) is salty, so go easy.

steps.title: Add the soya
steps.body: Stir the squeezed soya chunks into the sauce and simmer 3–4 min so they drink up the flavour.

steps.title: Toss it together
steps.body: Add the drained pasta to the sauce with a splash of the reserved pasta water. Toss over low heat until every piece is coated and glossy, loosening with more pasta water as needed.

steps.title: Serve
steps.body: Plate up, top with grated parmesan and torn basil if you have them, and eat hot.

notes: Forgot to save pasta water? Fake it: stir 1/4 tsp salt + 1/4 tsp cornflour into 1 cup hot water and use that — the starch helps the sauce cling. Or swirl a little hot water around the empty pasta pot to pick up the leftover starch. If the sauce is just thin, simmer it down or finish with a knob of butter.
Only have mustard oil? It works — heat it until it just smokes to kill the pungency, then lower the heat before adding garlic. Finish with a little butter or cream if the flavour feels too sharp.
For 4 people, roughly double everything: 8–10 tomatoes and 400–450 g pasta.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
